text:
The bridge at Ashwood is out, has been since the spring floods apparently, and no one thought to mention it in Brennmoor. Forded the river instead, a mile upstream where it narrows. Lost a boot to the current and half my dry rations.

Camped in the ruin of the old toll house. Someone had been there before me, recently, a fire pit still faintly warm, and a boot print too small to be mine.
